texto: Oh, but I was an idiot. Wanting to be whatever magic she waited for, when I had no magic - only darkness or death to give. But it seemed in that one instant, when she turned to discover what was behind her, that I could have brought happiness to at least one mortal. Me and my dreams of goodness. I had always been a fool for them.
explicacao: Citação de Krisi Keley sobre temas ligados a motivacao. “Oh, but I was an idiot. Wanting to be whatever magic she waited for, when I had no magic - only d…” articula motivacao, goodness, esperanca em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como luto e reflexao-noturna.

frase_original: Oh, but I was an idiot. Wanting to be whatever magic she waited for, when I had no magic - only darkness or death to give. But it seemed in that one instant, when she turned to discover what was behind her, that I could have brought happiness to at least one mortal. Me and my dreams of goodness. I had always been a fool for them.

texto: You are unwilling to pay that price, even knowing that the consolation prize is not only to learn every philosophy that has ever existed, but ones which have not yet been conceived? Even knowing that if you do not accept, you will soon cease to learn anything at all?"Raimund tilted his head, still staring into my eyes, and I knew he must see the tears filling them, though I held them back from falling."My friend," he whispered, "do you really believe your own words, I wonder? Your pain makes me think you know that death is not the end of learning, but only the beginning.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Krisi Keley que provoca esforço, persistência e resultados. Ao questionar “You are unwilling to pay that price, even knowing that the consolation prize is not only to learn…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre reflexao, death, knowledge, com foco em luto e termino.

Even knowing that if you do not accept, you will soon cease to learn anything at all?"Raimund tilted his head, still staring into my eyes, and I knew he must see the tears filling them, though I held them back from falling."My friend," he whispered, "do you really believe your own words, I wonder? Your pain makes me think you know that death is not the end of learning, but only the beginning.

texto: You understand nothing," I told him with a weary shake of the head, but I would not try to make him understand. That there was no justification for it: the murder of another, no matter how vile. We had all been wrong and, blackest of ironies, I had known this to hold that precious and wondrous thing, life, in my hands. To hold it in my hands before I destroyed it.

texto: There is fact in every fiction and truth in every lie.
explicacao: Citação de Krisi Keley sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “There is fact in every fiction and truth in every lie.” articula reflexao, fiction, verdade em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como verdade.
